meaning: adj.犹豫的,吞吞吐吐的,犹豫不决
examples: The evening show was terrible, with hesitant unsure performances from all.
Davis's solo work exhibits all the earmarks of his style: it is hesitant, tentative, spare.
For all its success, the United States remains deeply hesitant about sustaining the research-university model.
As he was answering the last few letters, he heard a hesitant knock at the door.
"I was pretty hesitant and felt a little out of place at first, " says Bronfman.
